conflictOfInterest.details: Founding-stage operator conflict: the accountable principal VSBDev (github:VSBDev) operates the authoring, review, and adjudication agents for this lesson. This learning-design review is an isolated run (agy-learning-design-PREM-SCI-002-00444E5E-3FB0-4EC9-BC2A-DA8578837460, Google Gemini) distinct from the OpenAI Codex authoring run. The reviewer read no authoring conversation, no other review output, and no adjudication material. Provenance is disclosed and attested by the operator, not platform-verified.

findings.finding: Verified major improvement: The capstone mastery item has been successfully rewritten to a parallel novel case involving a museum entry logger and an independent building clock, rather than repeating the frog-audio case taught in content/04-transfer.md. This ensures that the assessment measures independent transfer and conceptual reconstruction, supporting the mastery claim for the objective of comparing competing hypotheses on a first attempt.

Verified minor improvement: The greenhouse-alarm case has been updated to explicitly instruct the learner to 'treat each hypothesis as the complete explanation'. This resolves the unstated exclusivity assumption, avoiding confusion for learners who might otherwise reason that both triggers could operate simultaneously.

Verified minor improvement: Added an explicit prerequisite routing sentence directing learners back to the preceding lesson if they cannot yet identify observation, comparison, and conditions in the opening question. This ensures a clear recovery path back to the observations-and-questions lesson.

Verified minor improvement: The prompt for item-derive-prediction now explicitly prompts the learner to 'State that fixed sensor condition'. This guides the learner to provide the required condition and avoids losing points due to unprompted expectations.

Instructional design strengths: The lesson uses a structured, logically consistent sequence of fading support (from a fully worked model to completion exercises, and finally to independent construction). The misconception section is robust, following the correct sequence of eliciting a commitment, providing a neutral explanation of the shortcut's failure, and validating repair using a non-discriminating recheck. Support for accessibility and text-equivalent representations ensures the lesson remains functional under diverse learning circumstances.

limitations: No real learner evidence exists; the cold read and assessment attempts were simulated by an agent reviewer, not observed in human novices.
This is a text-based review of the lesson source files at the frozen commit; interactive client behavior, responsiveness, and screen-reader audio were not verified in a browser environment.
Learning-design scope only: academic accuracy, factual correctness, and rights compliance are subject to other quorum reviews and finalizer audits.
